meaning: vt.使重新着手,改写受信人姓名地址
examples: Bina believes that brands need to readdress their exclusivity tactic.
Instead of insisting that you hash it out at that moment, schedule a time to readdress the issue.
Hansson added that the findings would help to 'readdress the balance between economic needs, health, and the costs of working days lost'.
So, why not get ahead of the problem and readdress this when Congress reconvenes next year?
Membership of the euro means they don't have the option of allowing their currency to depreciate to readdress that problem.
